  • Assignment 4
    • Text Analytics using quanteda
    • 1. Read tutorial of quanteda
    • 2a. Use sample programs to analyze US presidential inaugural speeches
      • Similarities and differences among presidents
      • Similarities and differences over time
    • 2b. Use sample programs to analyze US Department of Defense reports
    • 3. What is Wordfish?
    • 3a. Run Wordfish on DOD reports
    • 4. How to compare positions

Assignment 4

Author

Soha Arian

Assignment 4

Text Analytics using quanteda

1. Read tutorial of quanteda

The quanteda tutorial provides the workflow used in this assignment: corpus, tokens, and document-feature matrix.

2a. Use sample programs to analyze US presidential inaugural speeches

This section uses the built-in inaugural speech corpus in quanteda.

library(quanteda)
library(quanteda.textmodels)
library(quanteda.textstats)
library(quanteda.textplots)
library(readtext)
library(ggplot2)

theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(color = "#d63384", face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
      axis.title = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
      axis.text = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
      legend.title = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
      legend.text = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
      strip.text = element_text(color = "#d63384", face = "bold")
    )
)

packageVersion("quanteda")
[1] '4.3.1'
corp_inaug <- data_corpus_inaugural
summary(corp_inaug, n = 5)
Corpus consisting of 60 documents, showing 5 documents:

            Text Types Tokens Sentences Year  President FirstName
 1789-Washington   625   1537        23 1789 Washington    George
 1793-Washington    96    147         4 1793 Washington    George
      1797-Adams   826   2577        37 1797      Adams      John
  1801-Jefferson   717   1923        41 1801  Jefferson    Thomas
  1805-Jefferson   804   2380        45 1805  Jefferson    Thomas
                 Party
                  none
                  none
            Federalist
 Democratic-Republican
 Democratic-Republican
head(docvars(corp_inaug))
  Year  President FirstName                 Party
1 1789 Washington    George                  none
2 1793 Washington    George                  none
3 1797      Adams      John            Federalist
4 1801  Jefferson    Thomas Democratic-Republican
5 1805  Jefferson    Thomas Democratic-Republican
6 1809    Madison     James Democratic-Republican
toks_inaug <- tokens(
  corp_inaug,
  remove_punct = TRUE,
  remove_numbers = TRUE,
  remove_symbols = TRUE
)

toks_inaug <- tokens_remove(toks_inaug, pattern = stopwords("en"))

dfmat_inaug <- dfm(toks_inaug)
dfmat_inaug
Document-feature matrix of: 60 documents, 9,359 features (92.75% sparse) and 4 docvars.
                 features
docs              fellow-citizens senate house representatives among
  1789-Washington               1      1     2               2     1
  1793-Washington               0      0     0               0     0
  1797-Adams                    3      1     0               2     4
  1801-Jefferson                2      0     0               0     1
  1805-Jefferson                0      0     0               0     7
  1809-Madison                  1      0     0               0     0
                 features
docs              vicissitudes incident life event filled
  1789-Washington            1        1    1     2      1
  1793-Washington            0        0    0     0      0
  1797-Adams                 0        0    2     0      0
  1801-Jefferson             0        0    1     0      0
  1805-Jefferson             0        0    2     0      0
  1809-Madison               0        0    1     0      1
[ reached max_ndoc ... 54 more documents, reached max_nfeat ... 9,349 more features ]

Similarities and differences among presidents

dfmat_pres <- dfm_group(dfmat_inaug, groups = President)
topfeatures(dfmat_pres, 20)
    people government         us        can       must       upon      great 
       592        575        507        489        377        371        354 
       may     states      world     nation    country      shall      every 
       343        343        329        324        323        316        309 
       one      peace        new      power        now     public 
       274        259        256        246        238        227 
pres_sim <- textstat_simil(dfmat_pres, method = "cosine")
as.matrix(pres_sim)
               Adams     Biden  Buchanan      Bush    Carter Cleveland

Similarities and differences over time

era <- ifelse(docvars(corp_inaug, "Year") < 1900, "Before1900", "1900plus")
dfmat_era <- dfm_group(dfmat_inaug, groups = era)
topfeatures(dfmat_era, 20)
    people government         us        can       must       upon      great 
       592        575        507        489        377        371        354 
       may     states      world     nation    country      shall      every 
       343        343        329        324        323        316        309 
       one      peace        new      power        now     public 
       274        259        256        246        238        227 
key_era <- textstat_keyness(dfmat_era, target = "1900plus")
head(key_era, 20)
     feature      chi2            p n_target n_reference
1    america 180.67824 0.000000e+00      209          11
2      world 170.93407 0.000000e+00      282          47
3        new 114.94895 0.000000e+00      213          43
4         us 104.59088 0.000000e+00      367         140
5      today 104.53034 0.000000e+00      112           3
6  americans  90.97920 0.000000e+00       90           0
7    freedom  77.95762 0.000000e+00      154          34
8       know  76.57555 0.000000e+00      102          10
9       work  64.33573 9.992007e-16      107          18
10      must  63.91212 1.332268e-15      265         112
11  together  55.91312 7.571721e-14       83          11
12    nation  52.04095 5.435652e-13      226          98
13 democracy  51.06374 8.941736e-13       64           5
14      help  48.82172 2.803202e-12       54           2
15      life  47.40356 5.777823e-12      111          30
16     thank  45.45907 1.558620e-11       45           0
17       way  45.25572 1.729139e-11       76          13
18  economic  44.55332 2.475242e-11       47           1
19       old  43.73156 3.766498e-11       68          10
20       let  40.36650 2.105205e-10      121          41
textplot_keyness(
  key_era,
  color = c("#d63384", "#f3bfd7"),
  labelcolor = "#d63384"
) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(color = "#d63384", face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "#d63384")
  )

2b. Use sample programs to analyze US Department of Defense reports

This section reads the DOD reports from the USDOD folder.

dod_texts <- readtext("USDOD/*.pdf")
dod_texts
readtext object consisting of 24 documents and 0 docvars.
# A data frame: 24 × 2
  doc_id                       text               
  <chr>                        <chr>              
1 1999_GPO-CRPT.pdf            "\"  U.S. NAT\"..."
2 2000_asia_neac_dod_china.pdf "\"          \"..."
3 2001_DoD_AR.pdf              "\"Annual Rep\"..."
4 2002_0712china.pdf           "\"          \"..."
5 2003_chinaex.pdf             "\"          \"..."
6 2004_0528PRC.pdf             "\"          \"..."
# ℹ 18 more rows
corp_dod <- corpus(dod_texts)
summary(corp_dod)
Corpus consisting of 24 documents, showing 24 documents:

                                                                                Text
                                                                   1999_GPO-CRPT.pdf
                                                        2000_asia_neac_dod_china.pdf
                                                                     2001_DoD_AR.pdf

                                                                    2004_0528PRC.pdf
                                                                  2005_0719china.pdf

                                                           2014_DoD_China_Report.pdf
                                                2015_China_Military_Power_Report.pdf
                                                2016 China Military Power Report.pdf
                                                2017_China_Military_Power_Report.pdf
                                                2018_CHINA-MILITARY-POWER-REPORT.pdf
                                                2019_CHINA_MILITARY_POWER_REPORT.pdf
                                      2020_DOD-CHINA-MILITARY-POWER-REPORT-FINAL.pdf
                                                                 2021-cmpr-final.pdf
 2022-military-and-security-developments-involving-the-peoples-republic-of-china.pdf
 Types Tokens Sentences
 16068 301499     10250
  2990  15078       540
 14864 186149      6246
  4178  25908      1051
  4068  25848       957
  4056  25091       876
  3730  20326       746
  4002  21855       642
  3669  18144       498
  4651  26390       679
  5124  31910       896
  5002  33621       768
  5400  35578       933
  3156  14293       346
  4955  30563       821
  4960  31111       850
  5335  34712       927
  5524  38929      1009
  5080  33380       912
  5759  45684      1201
  5868  45760      1143
  7373  74334      2341
  7727  79375      2400
  8086  83275      2536
toks_dod <- tokens(
  corp_dod,
  remove_punct = TRUE,
  remove_numbers = TRUE,
  remove_symbols = TRUE
)

toks_dod <- tokens_remove(toks_dod, pattern = stopwords("en"))

dfmat_dod <- dfm(toks_dod)
dfmat_dod_trim <- dfm_trim(dfmat_dod, min_termfreq = 5)

dfmat_dod_trim
Document-feature matrix of: 24 documents, 9,683 features (60.37% sparse) and 0 docvars.
                              features
docs                            u.s national security military commercial
  1999_GPO-CRPT.pdf            1676      510      701      468        294
  2000_asia_neac_dod_china.pdf    2       54       32       99          7
  2001_DoD_AR.pdf               656      323      302      718        139
  2002_0712china.pdf             35       65       44      192         10
  2003_chinaex.pdf               41       69       48      189         13
  2004_0528PRC.pdf               57       58       49      212          6
                              features
docs                           concerns people’s republic china volume
  1999_GPO-CRPT.pdf                  90       93       88   645    416
  2000_asia_neac_dod_china.pdf        4        9        3   160      0

  2002_0712china.pdf                  6        5        3   287      0
  2003_chinaex.pdf                    8        7        2   239      0
  2004_0528PRC.pdf                   15        7        3   199      1
[ reached max_ndoc ... 18 more documents, reached max_nfeat ... 9,673 more features ]
topfeatures(dfmat_dod_trim, 30)
       china     military      defense      china’s          prc          pla 
        9782         8586         6408         5564         5328         4916 
    security          u.s        force   operations       forces       states 
        4278         3917         3433         3061         3033         2942

        2366         2304         2298         2292         2239         2194 
   committee      support  development     aircraft          new      missile 
        2185         2144         2122         1993         1977         1949 
dod_sim <- textstat_simil(dfmat_dod_trim, method = "cosine")
as.matrix(dod_sim)

3. What is Wordfish?

Wordfish is an unsupervised one-dimensional text scaling method that estimates document positions from observed word frequencies.

3a. Run Wordfish on DOD reports

wf_dod <- textmodel_wordfish(dfmat_dod_trim, dir = c(1, ndoc(dfmat_dod_trim)))
summary(wf_dod)

Call:
textmodel_wordfish.dfm(x = dfmat_dod_trim, dir = c(1, ndoc(dfmat_dod_trim)))

Estimated Document Positions:
                                                                                        theta
1999_GPO-CRPT.pdf                                                                   -4.057564
2000_asia_neac_dod_china.pdf                                                        -0.218192

wf_plot_data <- data.frame(
  document = docnames(dfmat_dod_trim),
  wordfish_position = as.numeric(wf_dod$theta)
)

ggplot(wf_plot_data, aes(x = wordfish_position, y = reorder(document, wordfish_position))) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#d63384") +
  labs(
    title = "Wordfish Positions for DOD Reports",
    x = "Wordfish Position",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(color = "#d63384", face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "#d63384")
  )
dod_wordfish_positions <- data.frame(
  document = docnames(dfmat_dod_trim),
  wordfish_position = as.numeric(wf_dod$theta)
)

knitr::kable(dod_wordfish_positions, digits = 3, format = "html")
document wordfish_position
1999_GPO-CRPT.pdf -4.058
2000_asia_neac_dod_china.pdf -0.218
2001_DoD_AR.pdf -1.757
2002_0712china.pdf -0.329

2004_0528PRC.pdf -0.115
2005_0719china.pdf 0.007

2012_CMPR_Final.pdf 0.320

2014_DoD_China_Report.pdf 0.395
2015_China_Military_Power_Report.pdf 0.431
2016 China Military Power Report.pdf 0.512
2017_China_Military_Power_Report.pdf 0.565
2018_CHINA-MILITARY-POWER-REPORT.pdf 0.603

2022-military-and-security-developments-involving-the-peoples-republic-of-china.pdf 0.611

4. How to compare positions

Positions can be compared by examining the estimated document scores from Wordfish and comparing them to another scaling method such as correspondence analysis.

ca_dod <- textmodel_ca(dfmat_dod_trim)
dod_positions_compare <- data.frame(
  document = docnames(dfmat_dod_trim),
  wordfish = as.numeric(wf_dod$theta),
  ca_dim1 = as.numeric(coef(ca_dod, doc_dim = 1)$coef_document)
)

knitr::kable(dod_positions_compare, digits = 3, format = "html")
document wordfish ca_dim1
1999_GPO-CRPT.pdf -4.058 -1.893
2000_asia_neac_dod_china.pdf -0.218 0.449
2001_DoD_AR.pdf -1.757 0.216
2002_0712china.pdf -0.329 0.435
2003_chinaex.pdf -0.191 0.493
2004_0528PRC.pdf -0.115 0.543
2005_0719china.pdf 0.007 0.551
2006_dod.pdf 0.070 0.552
2007_dod.pdf 0.110 0.564
2008_dod.pdf 0.154 0.561
2009_China_Military_Power_Report.pdf 0.173 0.561
2010_CMPR_Final.pdf 0.224 0.533
2011_CMPR_Final.pdf 0.283 0.587
2012_CMPR_Final.pdf 0.320 0.539
2013_China_Report_FINAL.pdf 0.348 0.579
2014_DoD_China_Report.pdf 0.395 0.597
2015_China_Military_Power_Report.pdf 0.431 0.594
2016 China Military Power Report.pdf 0.512 0.649
2017_China_Military_Power_Report.pdf 0.565 0.670
2018_CHINA-MILITARY-POWER-REPORT.pdf 0.603 0.674
2019_CHINA_MILITARY_POWER_REPORT.pdf 0.604 0.657
2020_DOD-CHINA-MILITARY-POWER-REPORT-FINAL.pdf 0.627 0.623
2021-cmpr-final.pdf 0.632 0.575
2022-military-and-security-developments-involving-the-peoples-republic-of-china.pdf 0.611 0.559 
ggplot(dod_positions_compare, aes(x = wordfish, y = ca_dim1, label = document)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#d63384") +
  geom_text(size = 3, vjust = -0.5, check_overlap = TRUE, color = "#d63384") +
  labs(
    title = "Comparing Document Positions",
    x = "Wordfish Position",
    y = "Correspondence Analysis Dimension 1"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(color = "#d63384", face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "#d63384"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "#d63384")
  )
cor(dod_positions_compare$wordfish, dod_positions_compare$ca_dim1)
[1] 0.9366541

AI Disclaimer: I used ChatGPT as a tutor to clarify instructions, troubleshoot errors, and better understand mistakes encountered during the process. It also assisted with wording and formatting; however, the final submission reflects my own work and judgment.
